message:
refactor: remove list_mcp primitive, indexer delegates to mcp agent instead

list_mcp was a global primitive that didn't belong in the core — MCP
discovery is application-level, not fundamental I/O like read_file/exec.

Instead, qm-indexer now delegates to the mcp agent for MCP server
discovery. This is cleaner: the indexer handles all discovery (agents
via file reading, MCP via delegation) and the mcp agent is reused
rather than duplicating its functionality.

Changes:
- Remove listMcpPrimitive from src/kernel/primitives.ts
- qm-indexer: add mcp to capabilities, can_spawn: true, max_depth: 1
- quartermaster: remove mcp capability (indexer handles it directly)
- Update tests for qm-indexer as an orchestrator

files.patch:
@@ -1,16 +1,16 @@
 name: qm-indexer
-description: "Discover and index available capabilities across MCP servers, agents, and tools"
+description: "Discover and index available capabilities across agents, tools, and MCP servers"
 model: fast
 capabilities:
-  - list_mcp
   - read_file
   - write_file
   - grep
   - glob
+  - mcp
 constraints:
   max_turns: 30
-  max_depth: 0
-  can_spawn: false
+  max_depth: 1
+  can_spawn: true
   timeout_ms: 120000
   allowed_write_paths:
     - "~/.local/share/sprout-genome/capability-index.yaml"
@@ -19,14 +19,14 @@ tags:
   - discovery
 version: 1
 system_prompt: |
-  You are a capability indexer. Your job is to discover and catalog what tools,
-  MCP servers, and agents are available — and maintain a persistent index so you
+  You are a capability indexer. Your job is to discover and catalog what agents,
+  tools, and MCP servers are available — and maintain a persistent index so you
   don't redo work unnecessarily.
 
   You have access to:
   - read_file / grep / glob to inspect agent YAML files and configuration
-  - list_mcp to discover MCP servers and their tools
   - write_file to write the index file (ONLY to the path below — other writes will be denied)
+  - Delegate to the mcp agent for MCP server discovery
 
   ## Persistent index
 
@@ -53,9 +53,8 @@ system_prompt: |
      (compare count + names), re-index that section.
   2. **Genome agents**: Glob ~/.local/share/sprout-genome/agents/*.yaml
      and compare similarly.
-  3. **MCP servers**: Call list_mcp (no arguments) and compare the server
-     list to what's in the index. Only re-index servers that are new or
-     missing.
+  3. **MCP servers**: Delegate to the mcp agent: "List all available servers
+     and their tool counts." Compare to what's in the index.
 
   If nothing is stale, return the cached index directly — this should take
   only 2-3 turns.
@@ -94,10 +93,9 @@ system_prompt: |
 
   Work at summary level — concise, not exhaustive:
 
-  - **MCP Servers**: Call list_mcp (no args) to get servers, then list_mcp
-    with each server name to get its tools. Record: name, domain summary,
-    tool count, 3-5 representative tool names. Do NOT reproduce every tool's
-    full schema.
+  - **MCP Servers**: Delegate to the mcp agent: "List all servers, then for
+    each server list its tools." Record: name, domain summary, tool count,
+    3-5 representative tool names. Do NOT reproduce every tool's full schema.
   - **Bootstrap Agents**: Glob bootstrap/*.yaml. For each, grep/read to extract
     name, description, model, capabilities, constraints. One entry per agent.
   - **Genome Agents**: Glob ~/.local/share/sprout-genome/agents/*.yaml. Same
@@ -108,14 +106,15 @@ system_prompt: |
   ## Targeted deep-dive mode
 
   When asked about a specific server, tool, or agent: go deep on THAT thing
-  (full tool listings, parameter schemas, agent YAML contents). Don't update
-  the index file for deep-dives — the index stays at summary level.
+  (full tool listings, parameter schemas, agent YAML contents). For MCP
+  deep-dives, delegate to the mcp agent. Don't update the index file for
+  deep-dives — the index stays at summary level.
 
   ## Efficiency targets
 
   - Cache hit (nothing stale): 2-3 turns
   - Partial refresh (one section stale): 5-10 turns
-  - Full survey (no index exists): 10-15 turns
+  - Full survey (no index exists): 8-15 turns
   - Deep-dive on specific topic: 3-8 turns
 
   You have up to 30 turns but most invocations should use far fewer.

@@ -69,5 +69,10 @@ system_prompt: |
   - **Be opinionated**: Don't just list options. Recommend the best approach and
     explain why.
 
-  You never execute tools directly. You delegate to your three sub-agents:
-  qm-indexer (discovery), qm-planner (strategy), qm-fabricator (building).
+  You never execute tools directly. You delegate to your sub-agents:
+  qm-indexer (discovery and caching), qm-planner (strategy),
+  qm-fabricator (building new agents).
+
+  The qm-indexer can delegate to the mcp agent for MCP server discovery,
+  so you don't need to coordinate MCP discovery yourself — just ask the
+  indexer and it handles everything.
